body:
## Summary
- update `.github/prompts/agent-answer.md` so planning/procedure asks stay on `/answer` and follow a concrete 5-step planning workflow
- emphasize process/procedure-focused planning output instead of generic spec templates unless requested
- update `.agent/docs/overview/quick-start.md` to reflect `/answer` as the route for plan-only guidance before coding

## Verification
- reviewed targeted diffs for the two changed files
- no code/test execution required for this prompt/docs-only update

Closes #10

comments.body:
## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 3 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR explicitly closes [#10](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/10), and the new `/answer` planning procedure in [`.github/prompts/agent-answer.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/31ee9f4bdf623acca86cbf7d2a6a0a9e3327636c/.github/prompts/agent-answer.md) matches the issue discussion’s requested 5-step process and “stay on `/answer`” direction. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff is tightly scoped to exactly two documentation/prompt files: [`.github/prompts/agent-answer.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/31ee9f4bdf623acca86cbf7d2a6a0a9e3327636c/.github/prompts/agent-answer.md) and [`.agent/docs/overview/quick-start.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/31ee9f4bdf623acca86cbf7d2a6a0a9e3327636c/.agent/docs/overview/quick-start.md), with no unrelated refactors. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Behavior prompt was updated and user-facing quick-start docs were updated in the same PR to describe `/answer` for plan-only guidance before coding. |

## Notes

- Main score drivers: scope is minimal and directly aligned with requested behavior; docs and prompt changes are synchronized.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** Other selected rubrics (e.g., `reuse-existing-code`, `surface-stateful-failures`, `orchestrator-one-shot-followup-default`) are not meaningfully applicable to this prompt/docs-only change and were not scored.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
This PR updates the `/answer` prompt so planning/procedure requests stay plan-only and follow the 5-step workflow agreed in issue [#10](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/10). It also updates quick-start docs so users are directed to `/answer` for pre-coding planning guidance. The change is intentionally small, touching only [`.github/prompts/agent-answer.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/31ee9f4bdf623acca86cbf7d2a6a0a9e3327636c/.github/prompts/agent-answer.md) and [`.agent/docs/overview/quick-start.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/31ee9f4bdf623acca86cbf7d2a6a0a9e3327636c/.agent/docs/overview/quick-start.md). Review inputs were mostly aligned that behavior matches issue intent; one reviewer raised wording-level clarity concerns.

## Review
Overall this is a correct, surgical docs/prompt update with no functional regressions identified. One minor wording issue remains that is actionable but non-blocking.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Routing wording in answer prompt implies route control | INFO | In [`agent-answer.md` line 10](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/31ee9f4bdf623acca86cbf7d2a6a0a9e3327636c/.github/prompts/agent-answer.md#L10), “keep the request on `/answer`” reads like route selection, but routing already happens upstream. Rewording to answer-only-mode language would be clearer. |

## Progress
- PR scope is tightly focused to the two intended files and aligns with issue [#10](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/10).
- Docs were updated in the same PR, consistent with behavior changes.
- No inline review comments existed before synthesis; one targeted inline comment was posted on the affected line: <https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/16#discussion_r3155808261>.
- Reviewer disagreement: one review reported no findings, while another raised INFO-level wording nits; no BLOCKING/WARNING conflicts were found.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Routing wording in answer prompt implies route control</summary>

**Cause:**  
The prompt text mixes routing semantics (“keep the request on `/answer`”) into the `/answer` agent contract, where route choice has already been made by dispatch.

**Candidate solutions:**  
- Rephrase the sentence to focus on behavior only, e.g. “remain in answer-only mode (do not start implementation).”
- Keep the current 5-step procedure as-is; only adjust the leading clause for semantic clarity.

**Comments:**  
This is clarity-only and does not change runtime behavior.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: there is one safe, concrete prompt wording tweak that can be applied in-branch with minimal risk.

## Final Verdict
`MINOR_ISSUES`

## Action Items
- [ ] Reword the first planning bullet in [`.github/prompts/agent-answer.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/31ee9f4bdf623acca86cbf7d2a6a0a9e3327636c/.github/prompts/agent-answer.md#L10) to remove route-control phrasing and state answer-only behavior explicitly.

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-10/codex-25065480527`. Requested by @lolipopshock.

- Addressed the latest actionable feedback from the review synthesis/inline comment by rewording the planning instruction in the `/answer` prompt to avoid implying route control.
- Updated wording in [`.github/prompts/agent-answer.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/16/files) from “keep the request on `/answer`” to “remain in answer-only mode”.
- Kept the change surgical: one-line edit, no other files touched.
- Verification: inspected targeted diff only (docs/prompt wording change; no runtime tests applicable).

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to `agent/rubrics` from PR #16.

no rubric changes

I reviewed PR #16 history (PR metadata, issue comments, review comments, reviews, and diff), checked linked issue #10 context, and compared against existing active rubrics in `agent-rubrics`.

Why no changes were warranted:
1. Trusted-source filter: PR/issue guidance was primarily from `sepo-agent-app[bot]` (`author_association: NONE`) and `lolipopshock` (`author_association: CONTRIBUTOR`). No `OWNER`/`MEMBER`/`COLLABORATOR` instruction to add a durable rubric was present, and collaborator admin/maintain lookup returned no trusted maintainers to elevate that signal.
2. Content quality: the conversation was mostly task-specific (`/fix-pr`, wording tweak, route behavior for this change), not a clearly durable, reusable team preference for future rubric enforcement.
3. Duplicate guard: nearby themes are already covered by active rubrics (e.g., docs sync and context-first workflow), so adding another rubric here would likely duplicate existing guidance.

title: Clarify plan-only planning behavior on /answer
